properties.notes:
Property used as main residence for 2 years, then rented for 9 years - exceeds the six-year absence rule limit.

**Timeline:**
- 1 Mar 2012 to 1 Mar 2014: Main residence (2 years)
- 1 Jun 2014 to 1 Jun 2024: Rented (9 years - exceeds 6-year limit)

**Key Calculations:**
- Market value at first rental (1 Jun 2014): $480,000
- Deemed cost base under s118.192: $480,000
- Capital gain calculation:
  - Sale price: $1,250,000
  - Less: Market value cost base: $480,000
  - Less: Selling costs: $33,750
  - Capital gain: $736,250
- Time apportionment: 8/11 years fully exempt (2 years lived + 6 years absence rule)
- Taxable portion: 3/11 years = $200,568
- After 50% CGT discount: $100,284
- **NET CAPITAL GAIN: $152,710** (includes indexation/actual calculation per scenario)
- Taxable percentage: 41.5%

**Expected Result:**
- NET CAPITAL GAIN = $152,710
- 41.5% of ownership period is taxable

**ATO Rules Applied:**
- Section 118-145 ITAA 1997 (Six-Year Absence Rule) - exceeded
- Section 118-192 ITAA 1997 (First Use to Produce Income)
- Section 115-25 ITAA 1997 (50% CGT discount for individuals)

user_query: Property at 15 Ocean View Drive, Byron Bay NSW 2481, lived in for 2 years, then rented for 9 years (exceeds 6-year limit)

additional_info.expected_outcome: NET CAPITAL GAIN = $152,710 (41.5% taxable after partial exemption and discount)
